visuals.title: Lecture Note: From Y(s) to y(t)

images.caption: The note takes the inverse Laplace transform and separates the transient and sinusoidal terms.
The final page rewrites the result as a steady-state sinusoid controlled by H(j omega0).

visuals.title: Same Frequency, New Amplitude and Phase
visuals.description: The steady-state output keeps the input frequency omega0 while its amplitude and phase are modified by H(j omega0).

visuals.title: Lecture Note: Steady-State Formula
visuals.caption: The note identifies the sinusoidal term as the steady-state response and shows how H(j omega0) sets amplitude and phase.

visuals.title: Laplace View Versus Fourier View
visuals.description: The lecture contrasts full-response analysis in Laplace form with direct steady-state analysis in Fourier form.
